prompt: Photo-realistic editorial café still-life shot from above. Keep the existing scene exactly as-is: the blue marble bistro table with gilded scalloped edge, the cappuccino with latte art and biscuit on a white saucer with spoon, the bentwood cane café chair, the grey cobblestone street, the warm natural daylight and shallow depth of field. Preserve the original coffee, table, chair and lighting pixels. Only two things change: replace the canvas tote bag, and replace the flowers. The bag is a vintage caramel leather doctor's travel bag with a hinged frame top and twin handles, placed on the chair. The flowers are an understated handful of garden greenery and herbs — sprigs of rosemary, a few white cosmos — fresh and everyday. Render with authentic phone-camera realism: natural skin of imperfection in materials, real fabric grain, true-to-life shadows and soft window light, slight lens vignetting, no over-smoothing, no plastic sheen, no warped geometry, correct number of straps and handles, legible undistorted leather stitching, no extra limbs or duplicated objects, no text artifacts. Keep colors true — do not push the frame warm or pink.
